name: Brussel Sprouts and Chestnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the Brussels sprouts: Blanch them in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then run cold water over them.
2. Prepare the chestnuts: Make an "X" on the top of each one with a sharp knife. Brush each chestnut with oil and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, or until they are tender. Shell the cooked chestnuts.
3. Mix the prepared Brussels sprouts, roasted chestnuts, rosemary, tarragon, cumin, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
4. Pour the chicken broth into the bowl with the vegetables and stir to combine.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake for 20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the vegetables are tender.
6. In a small saucepan, mix the cornstarch with some of the remaining chicken broth. Cook over medium heat until thickened.
7. Pour the thickened sauce over the baked vegetables and stir to coat.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
